subject: Mathematics
title: Ramanujan: The Self-Taught Genius
description: One-minute skill: Ramanujan: The Self-Taught Genius.

hook.text: A clerk in India with no formal training mailed theorems to Cambridge that stunned the world's best.

buildup.text: Srinivasa Ramanujan taught himself math from a single outdated textbook in a small Indian town.

discovery.text: His notebooks contained thousands of results—many unknown, some so deep they're still being explored.

twist.text: He struggled in England's cold climate and strict vegetarian diet. He fell gravely ill.

climax.text: He died at 32, leaving formulas that now appear in string theory and black hole physics.

punchline.text: A self-taught clerk wrote formulas a century ahead of science.

quiz.question: How did Ramanujan learn mathematics?
options: Largely self-taught from a single textbook
